title: Lab 27: At Job Scheduling
description: Schedule a one time task with at and verify that the at daemon is enabled

tasks: On client, create the user queue27 and set its password to cinder9.
On client, enable and start the atd service.
On client, as user queue27, schedule a one-time at job that appends the text AT27 OK to /home/queue27/at27.log two minutes from now.
hints: You can submit the job to at with standard input.
Use atq to verify that the job is queued.
checks: getent passwd queue27 >/dev/null && getent shadow queue27 | awk -F: 'length($2)>0 && $2 !~ /^(!!?|\*|LK|NP)$/' >/dev/null
systemctl is-enabled atd | grep -qx enabled && systemctl is-active atd | grep -qx active
task_titles: Create the user queue27 and set its password to...
Enable and start the atd service
schedule a one-time at job that appends the text...
solution_commands: echo 'echo AT27 OK >> /home/queue27/at27.log' | at now + 2 minutes
